name: rating-agent-workflow
description: Workflow for using the session rating scripts as a reusable scoring template.
content:
Rating workflow template:

1. Agent scans the current workspace for candidate data files. If files are raw/unstandardized, the agent inspects their schema and writes a project-specific conversion script to produce standardized rating data. Do not start AI scoring from raw files directly.

2. If data is split across many participant/session/source files, the agent must first consolidate them into one standardized rating input file. The consolidated file should normally be named rating_input.xlsx unless the user chooses another name. Preserve provenance columns such as source_file, source_row, participant_id, session_id, condition, or other useful metadata when available.

3. Standardized rating data must contain these columns by default: id, question, answer. Extra columns are preserved by the scoring script. The id column must be stable and unique in the consolidated file. If the agent cannot confidently map raw columns to id/question/answer, it must ask the user before writing the conversion script.

4. Generated files should go into one output directory by default, normally rating_output/ under the current workspace. Put rating_input.xlsx, rating_config.json, ai_scored.xlsx, ai_agreement.xlsx, expert_sample.xlsx, expert_agreement.xlsx, and any temporary conversion scripts/logs there unless the user chooses another directory. Before writing outputs, show the planned output directory and ask for confirmation. Do not scatter generated files in the workspace root unless the user explicitly confirms that location.

5. Scoring output format: AI scoring produces one scored table, normally rating_output/ai_scored.xlsx, preserving all standardized input columns and appending score columns named {dimension}_score_{model}. Agreement analysis produces rating_output/ai_agreement.xlsx. Expert sampling produces rating_output/expert_sample.xlsx. Expert-filled agreement produces rating_output/expert_agreement.xlsx.

6. Use the scripts and default config template from the current session directory, not similarly named scripts from the user's workspace. The session directory is provided in <session-metadata>. Required template files are rating_engine.py, agreement_analysis.py, and rating_config.template.json. Workspace files are data/config/output inputs unless the user explicitly asks to edit or replace the template scripts.

7. Use rating_config.template.json as the default scoring template. It contains the default creativity scoring dimensions 实用性 and 新颖性, adapted from the user's existing rating_template JSON. Do not commit real API keys; use api_key_file or api_key_env.

8. Before scoring, the agent must identify a usable Python environment for the current workspace. Prefer an existing project virtual environment such as venv/bin/python or .venv/bin/python over the system default. Verify required packages can import: pandas, requests, openpyxl. Do not modify or install into a Python environment without user approval.

9. Before scoring, the agent must check API availability from the user-provided API config and query available models. The agent must confirm every selected model is available. For AI-AI agreement, select at least two available text/chat models. If only one model is selected or available, clearly state that model-to-model agreement cannot be computed and ask the user whether to continue with one model, choose more models, or stop. Do not run AI scoring until API and model availability are verified.

10. When available models are numerous, the agent should ask whether the user has preferred models. If not, recommend 2-3 suitable mainstream available text/chat models and ask the user to confirm. Do not silently choose a single historical model when the goal includes multi-model agreement.

11. Before scoring, the agent must review the first 10 standardized data rows and check whether the data content fits the default 实用性/新颖性 scoring criteria. The agent must summarize the planned input file, row count, columns, selected Python interpreter, API status, selected models, number of models, whether AI-AI agreement is possible, rating dimensions, full global prompt or faithful summary, each dimension prompt, score format, batch size, parallel workers, output directory, and output paths. Then ask the user for explicit confirmation before running AI scoring.

12. The agent must not start AI scoring until the user explicitly confirms the full plan. If the user has not confirmed, stop after presenting the plan and questions. Ask whether scoring prompts, dimensions, models, API settings, Python environment, template script paths, data conversion, output directory, or output paths should be changed before running.

13. The agent writes rating_config.json from the session directory's rating_config.template.json plus user-confirmed edits. It should include input column names, api settings, models, rating dimensions, parallel settings, and expert_review settings. API keys should not be committed to GitHub; prefer api_key_file or api_key_env.

14. Run AI scoring only after confirmation, with the confirmed Python interpreter and the session directory script, for example:
/path/to/python /path/to/session/rating_engine.py --input rating_output/rating_input.xlsx --config rating_output/rating_config.json --output rating_output/ai_scored.xlsx

15. After AI scoring, compute scorer agreement with the session directory script only when there are at least two score columns for a dimension. If there is only one model and no expert scores, skip AI-AI agreement and explain why.
/path/to/python /path/to/session/agreement_analysis.py agreement --input rating_output/ai_scored.xlsx --config rating_output/rating_config.json --output rating_output/ai_agreement.xlsx

16. Generate an expert scoring sample with the session directory script:
/path/to/python /path/to/session/agreement_analysis.py sample-expert --input rating_output/ai_scored.xlsx --config rating_output/rating_config.json --output rating_output/expert_sample.xlsx

17. After the user fills expert scores, compute AI-expert or expert-expert agreement with the session directory script:
/path/to/python /path/to/session/agreement_analysis.py agreement --input rating_output/ai_scored.xlsx --expert-scores rating_output/expert_filled.xlsx --config rating_output/rating_config.json --output rating_output/expert_agreement.xlsx

18. Agreement analysis is generic: AI-AI, AI-expert, and expert-expert are all treated as pairwise agreement among score columns. Score columns follow {dimension}_score_{rater}.
